refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness, and environmental factors. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time achievement does not compromise safety constraints.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time across representative duty cycles at the customer site. Collect telemetry data (timestamps for load start, travel completion, dump completion) and validate against CT_target under agreed conditions. Establish baseline and regression testing protocols.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump operations within individual phase time budgets that sum to ≤ CT_target.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall provide operator control responsiveness sufficient to achieve CT_target without introducing safety-critical delays.
FR-2.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall enforce cycle time constraints that do not conflict with safety-critical interlocks or emergency stops.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (phase timestamps, total cycle duration, p95 statistics) for performance monitoring.

candidate_dps: DP-2a: Optimize vehicle hydraulic and drivetrain response times; implement predictive load sequencing and travel path optimization; tune operator control gains for responsiveness.
DP-2b: Implement real-time cycle time monitoring and adaptive duty cycle adjustment; use machine learning to predict and optimize phase durations based on site conditions.
DP-2c: Establish a hierarchical control architecture with vehicle-level cycle time budgets, teleoperation latency budgets, and safety-critical time margins; allocate time budgets to subsystems.
DP-2d: Integrate cycle time KPI tracking into the telemetry pipeline; establish automated alerts when p95 approaches CT_target threshold; enable operator feedback loops.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-2c: Establish a hierarchical control architecture with vehicle-level cycle time budgets, teleoperation latency budgets, and safety-critical time margins; allocate time budgets to subsystems. This DP is selected because it provides a systematic, decomposable approach to cycle time management that can be allocated across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. It enables clear ownership boundaries and measurable sub-targets. DP-2d (telemetry integration) is complementary and will be addressed in sub-level decomposition.

potential_sources: Vehicle cycle time budget depends on hydraulic response and drivetrain efficiency (Vehicle DP).
Teleoperation latency budget depends on network link quality and operator control loop (Teleoperation DP).
Safety-critical time margins depend on Safety Services interlocks and emergency stop response (Safety Services DP).
Coupling is managed via explicit time budget allocation and interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert).
coupling_check.notes: The hierarchical budget approach decouples subsystem optimization: each module can optimize within its time budget without requiring real-time coordination. Coupling is mediated through well-defined telemetry reporting and safety alert thresholds.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target and duty cycle are formally defined and stable; risk: vague or changing targets will invalidate time budgets.

Assumption: Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services can be independently optimized within allocated time budgets; risk: unforeseen interactions (e.g., safety interlocks blocking optimal travel paths) may require re-allocation.
Assumption: Telemetry infrastructure can reliably capture phase timestamps with sufficient precision (millisecond-level); risk: network jitter or sensor latency may obscure true cycle time.
Assumption: Operator skill and site conditions are within the agreed duty cycle envelope; risk: operator variability or extreme site conditions may exceed budgets.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-2c to explicitly define the time budget allocation methodology: (1) specify how CT_target is decomposed into vehicle phase budgets (load, travel, dump), teleoperation latency budget, and safety margin; (2) define the interface contracts (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry) that enforce budget compliance; (3) establish a feedback loop (via Telemetry) to detect budget violations and trigger corrective actions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module owns the end-to-end cycle time KPI and the hierarchical time budget allocation framework. It integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems and ensures that their combined performance meets the p95 ≤ CT_target acceptance criterion. The module is responsible for defining time budgets, monitoring aggregate cycle time telemetry, and coordinating corrective actions when budgets are exceeded.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution reports cycle time metrics (phase timestamps, total cycle duration, p95 statistics, budget utilization) to external monitoring and analytics systems. Enables Site Manager and Project Manager to verify KPI achievement.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives duty cycle specifications and CT_target from Site Manager or Project Manager. Tasking includes load characteristics, travel distance, site conditions, and acceptance criteria.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution coordinates with Safety Services to ensure that safety-critical interlocks do not violate cycle time budgets. Receives safety alerts and communicates time margin constraints to Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural system-level owner of the cycle time KPI. It contains the time budget allocation logic, aggregate telemetry collection, and cross-subsystem coordination. Minimal diffusion: only the Telemetry reporting interface extends beyond the module boundary.

Vehicle owns the load, travel, and dump phase execution and can optimize within its allocated time budget. However, cycle time KPI ownership and cross-subsystem budget coordination belong at the NGHE Solution level. Assigning to Vehicle would diffuse the KPI responsibility and complicate teleoperation and safety integration.

Teleoperation owns operator control responsiveness and latency optimization. However, it does not own vehicle phase execution or safety constraints. Assigning cycle time KPI to Teleoperation would create artificial coupling and obscure the vehicle's role in cycle time achievement.

Safety Services owns safety-critical interlocks and emergency response. Cycle time KPI is not a safety function; assigning it to Safety Services would conflate safety and performance objectives and risk safety compromise for schedule optimization.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner of the cycle time KPI because it is the only module with visibility and authority over all three subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and their time budget interactions. The module will define the time budget allocation framework, collect aggregate telemetry, and coordinate corrective actions. Sub-level decomposition will allocate specific time budgets to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, with clear interface contracts enforced via Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Telemetry.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution module may become a coordination bottleneck if time budget re-allocation requires frequent cross-subsystem negotiation. Mitigation: establish clear, stable time budgets and exception handling protocols.

Risk: Telemetry interface may not capture sufficient detail to diagnose cycle time violations (e.g., which phase exceeded budget). Mitigation: define detailed telemetry schema with phase-level timestamps and budget utilization metrics.

Risk: Safety Services may impose time margins that conflict with CT_target. Mitigation: establish safety-performance trade-off analysis during requirements refinement; ensure SafetyAlert interface includes margin negotiation.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine the NGHE Solution IM boundary to explicitly define: (1) the time budget allocation data structure (vehicle phase budgets, teleoperation latency budget, safety margin); (2) the telemetry schema for cycle time reporting (phase timestamps, budget utilization, p95 statistics); (3) the exception handling protocol when budgets are exceeded (e.g., alert Site Manager, trigger re-optimization, escalate to Safety Services if safety margin is compromised).

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR (achieve p95 ≤ CT_target with telemetry monitoring) is directly addressed by DP-2c (hierarchical time budget allocation). The DP provides a systematic, decomposable approach to cycle time management that can be verified through telemetry data collection and budget compliance monitoring. The DP does not over-constrain the solution (e.g., it does not mandate specific vehicle hydraulics or network protocols) and allows flexibility in sub-level optimization.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion risk: Time budget allocation logic may be distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules if sub-level DPs are not carefully coordinated. Mitigation: establish a centralized time budget registry in NGHE Solution and enforce interface contracts via Tasking and SafetyAlert.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution IM boundary cleanly contains the time budget allocation framework and aggregate telemetry collection. Sub-level decomposition will allocate specific time budgets to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, with clear ownership and interface contracts. No significant diffusion expected at this level.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI can be verified through telemetry data collection at the customer site. Measurement approach: collect phase timestamps (load start, travel completion, dump completion) across representative duty cycles, compute p95 cycle time, and compare against CT_target. Verification is feasible if telemetry infrastructure is reliable and duty cycle is well-defined.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The NGHE Solution module is clearly responsible for cycle time KPI achievement. Sub-level decomposition will allocate specific time budgets to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, with measurable acceptance criteria for each. Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Refine the Tasking interface to include formal duty cycle specification (load characteristics, travel distance, site conditions, CT_target) and acceptance criteria. Establish a change control process for duty cycle updates.
Refine the Telemetry interface to include detailed cycle time metrics (phase timestamps, budget utilization, p95 statistics, anomalies). Define telemetry schema and reporting frequency.
Establish a SafetyAlert interface protocol for safety-performance trade-off negotiation. Define how safety margins are allocated and how conflicts are resolved.
Define the time budget allocation methodology: specify how CT_target is decomposed into vehicle phase budgets, teleoperation latency budget, and safety margin. Document assumptions and trade-offs.

decision.justification: The refined FR and selected DP are well-formed and pass the gate review. The NGHE Solution IM boundary is appropriate and contains the cycle time KPI ownership with minimal diffusion. However, the DP requires sub-level decomposition to allocate time budgets to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services and to define the detailed interface contracts (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry). Sub-level decomposition is necessary to operationalize the hierarchical time budget framework and establish measurable acceptance criteria for each subsystem.
decision.justification_detail: The current level establishes the system-level cycle time KPI and the hierarchical budget allocation approach. Sub-level decomposition will address: (1) Vehicle cycle time optimization (load, travel, dump phase budgets); (2) Teleoperation latency and operator control responsiveness; (3) Safety Services time margin allocation and interlock timing; (4) Telemetry collection and reporting infrastructure. Each sub-level FR will be allocated to a specific module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) with clear ownership and interface contracts.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump operations within individual phase time budgets that sum to ≤ CT_target.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall provide operator control responsiveness sufficient to achieve CT_target without introducing safety-critical delays.
FR-2.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall enforce cycle time constraints that do not conflict with safety-critical interlocks or emergency stops.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (phase timestamps, total cycle duration, p95 statistics) for performance monitoring.

assumptions: CT_target and duty cycle are formally defined and stable.
Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services can be independently optimized within allocated time budgets.
Telemetry infrastructure can reliably capture phase timestamps with millisecond-level precision.
Operator skill and site conditions are within the agreed duty cycle envelope.
Safety-critical interlocks can be designed to respect cycle time budgets without compromising safety.
open_risks: Vague or changing CT_target and duty cycle definitions will invalidate time budgets.
Unforeseen interactions between vehicle optimization, teleoperation latency, and safety interlocks may require re-allocation of time budgets.
Network jitter or sensor latency may obscure true cycle time and complicate verification.
Operator variability or extreme site conditions may exceed budgets.
Safety Services may impose time margins that conflict with CT_target, requiring trade-off analysis.
evidence_needed: Formal duty cycle specification with load characteristics, travel distance, site conditions, and CT_target.
Baseline cycle time measurements from existing equipment or prototypes under agreed duty cycle.
Telemetry infrastructure design and validation (timestamp precision, reporting frequency, data quality).
Safety analysis documenting time margins required for safety-critical interlocks and emergency response.
Operator control responsiveness requirements and latency budgets for teleoperation.
Time budget allocation methodology and trade-off analysis (vehicle phases, teleoperation latency, safety margin).
